sourceMovie.title: Parasyte: Part 1
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Parasyte: Part 1 (2014) as a science fiction theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The science fiction and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Parasyte: Part 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Parasyte: Part 1 clicked for you, Parasyte: Part 2 (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Parasyte: Part 1 clicked for you, Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and action, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Parasyte: Part 1?
faq.answer: Try Parasyte: Part 2, Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone, Evangelion: 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance—each shares DNA with Parasyte: Part 1 while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Parasyte: Part 1?
faq.answer: Parasyte: Part 1 (2014) is best described as Science Fiction, Horror, Action, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Parasyte: Part 1 part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Parasyte Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
